mission.title: argo-events: SASL authentication to Kafka
mission.description: SASL authentication to Kafka. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-events deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-events version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
helm list -n argo-events 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-events installation.

steps.title: Review argo-events configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-events configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
kubectl get configmap -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-events
```
I may be doing something wrong, but I can't seem to find out how to make a SASL connection to a Kafka Event Source. 

It would be nice to have SASL authentication to a Kafka Event Source and Event Trigger, besides a TLS connection.

steps.title: Apply the fix for SASL authentication to Kafka
steps.description:
Checklist:

* [X] Fixes #703

Test Cases:
* [X] Test Invalid Credentials (EventSource and Trigger)
* [X] Test Invalid SASL Mechanism (EventSource and Trigger)
* [X] Test SASL PLAIN Mechanism

Notes:

- sarama library says that it only supports [SASL/PLAIN](https://github.com/Shopify/sarama/blob/a7eb541855c8e19965a30547eadc20f6bc6edea5/config.go#L60), but has different mechanisms available in its

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
kubectl get events -n argo-events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "SASL authentication to Kafka" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:

Test Cases:

Notes:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-events installed or the issue environment reproducible.
